name: Burayr ibn Khudhayr
title: Master of Quran Reciters
shortDescription: A prominent elder of Kufa and an outstanding Quran reciter who joked with companions on Ashura's eve out of eagerness for martyrdom.
fullStory:
Burayr ibn Khudhayr al-Mishra'i al-Hamdani was a noble, ascetic, and prominent elder of Kufa who taught and interpreted the Quran in the Grand Mosque of Kufa. Due to his unparalleled mastery of the Quran and his beautiful recitation, he was known among the people as 'Sayyid al-Qurra' (Master of the Reciters). He was considered one of the sincere Shias and loyal companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS).

Upon hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) movement towards Mecca, Burayr left Kufa, joined the Imam's caravan, and accompanied him to Karbala. Throughout the journey and during the difficult days of the siege, he was a firm supporter and a powerful orator of the caravan, trying to awaken the dormant conscience of the Kufan army using Quranic reasoning.

On the night of Ashura, Burayr's morale was so high and full of certainty that, unlike his usual serious and dignified demeanor, he joked and jested with Abd al-Rahman ibn Abd Rabbah. When Abd al-Rahman surprisingly told him that this was not the time for joking, Burayr replied with a smile: 'My people know that I was never a man of vanity or joking, neither in my youth nor in my old age. But by God, I am so happy about what we will meet tomorrow that I cannot contain myself. There is nothing between us and Paradise except that these people attack us with their swords, and how I wish that time would come sooner'.

On the day of Ashura, Burayr went to the battlefield after Hurr ibn Yazid and fought with rare bravery. During the battle, he severely reprimanded the Kufan army for blocking the water from the household of the Prophet. An enemy commander named Radhi ibn Munqidh engaged him, and Burayr threw him to the ground and sat on his chest. Seeing himself on the verge of death, Radhi called his comrades for help.

At this moment, Ka'b ibn Jabir attacked Burayr from behind, striking his back with a spear. Burayr fell to the ground and was martyred by Ka'b's sword. The martyrdom of this great reciter and teacher of the Quran left a heavy sorrow on the hearts of the Imam's companions and immortalized his name as one of the most mystical martyrs of Karbala.
quoteArabic: وَاللّهِ إنّی لَمُسْتَبْشِرٌ بِما نَحْنُ لاقُونَ
quote: By God, I am infinitely happy about the martyrdom we will meet tomorrow.
